genre_refs: law-enforcement 'End of Watch' / Wall of Honor memorial
description: A 'Wall of Honor' graphic in landscape format. The background is a dark blue slate or stone texture; on the right side is a photograph of a U.S. flag and a bouquet of deep-red roses. Upper left, large white serif type reads 'Wall of Honor' next to a small pin or ribbon emblem (a blue ribbon with the ICE/DHS shield). Below the title, white text reads 'Special Agent in Charge / Roger L. Von Amelunxen', then a thin gold divider, the phrase 'End of Watch' in serif with 'of' italic, and the date 'Wednesday, July 30, 1980.' An inset photo at center-right shows an antique gold law-enforcement badge reading 'U.S. CUSTOMS / THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY' with a Treasury seal, '1789', a banner 'PATROL OFFICER', and the number '271'.
notable_text: Wall of Honor; Special Agent in Charge Roger L. Von Amelunxen; End of Watch; Wednesday, July 30, 1980; U.S. CUSTOMS THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY; PATROL OFFICER
